LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SMITHGROUP INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED AND THE ALTERNATE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION AND GRANTED AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION. AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR OVERTIME, THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER THE LEARNED PROFESSIONAL EXEMPTION APPLIES. AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR WAITING TIME PENALTIES, THERE IS NO EVIDENCE OF WILLFULNESS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-12-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: TOMAS MARGAIN, ESQ., DAL BON & MARGAIN, 408.297.4729, FOR PLAINTIFF OSWALD CASTILLO.
GILBERT TSAI, ESQ., HANSON BRIDGETT, 415.777.3200, FOR DEFENDANT SMITH GROUP INC.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT SMITHGROUP INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied and the alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to the first cause of action and granted as to the second cause of action. As to the first cause of action for overtime, there is a triable issue whether the learned professional exemption applies. As to the second cause of action for waiting time penalties, there is no evidence of willfulness. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077.
